text.title: ai-governance-compliance Core Rules
text.summary: Migrated testable rules for ai-governance-compliance. Verify and refine before relying on for production.

rule.statement: Block any deployment where EU AI Act tier is `high` and the conformity assessment file is missing or incomplete.
rule.rationale: Article 16-21 require the technical file PRIOR to placing the system on the market. Post-hoc remediation is not legally permitted.

rule.statement: Audit log inserts MUST be atomic (write-then-rename for files, single INSERT for SQL); never write JSON line-by-line via streaming append without flush+fsync.
rule.rationale: Crashes mid-write produce truncated lines that break audit reconstruction; auditors reject corrupt logs.

rule.statement: Governance agents MUST NOT self-approve their own model deployments at high-risk tier; require an external human approver identity captured in the audit record.
rule.rationale: Article 14 (human oversight) and ISO/IEC 42001 require independent review before deployment of high-risk AI.

rule.statement: Bias evaluation MUST run on a held-out set drawn from the production data distribution, not the original benchmark dataset.
rule.rationale: Benchmark distributions diverge from production; benchmark fairness does not predict production fairness.

rule.statement: Disparate impact ratio must be > 0.8 OR a documented exception with sign-off from compliance officer must be on file.
rule.rationale: EEOC four-fifths rule and general fairness practice; below 0.8 = adverse impact requiring justification.

rule.statement: Sensitive features (race, gender, age, ethnicity) MUST NOT be used directly as model inputs unless explicitly required and justified in the risk assessment.
rule.rationale: Avoids direct discrimination; proxy detection done via SHAP feature-importance scan instead.
